feed.title: SAP Community - SAP Concur
feed.subtitle: SAP Concur Q&A in SAP Community

entry.title: Audit Trail in Concur Test gone for old reports
entry.content:
Hello,

Anyone can confirm the reason why audit trail for old reports in Concur TEST is wiped out/deleted? And we'd like to know if this is some setting that we can change and how long before they get deleted?

Appreciate all the help!

entry.title: SAP Concur Expense - Is it possible to add “Employee Preferred Name“ as searchable field?
entry.content:
Dear Experts,

Is it possible to add “Employee Preferred Name“, next to "Employee First name" and "Employee Last Name" when using find functionality upon searching reports?

entry.title: Combining Multiple Entry Comments into One Row in Concur Cognos Reports (Analysis/Intelligence)
entry.content:
Have you ever faced duplicate rows when fetching entry comments from Concur via Cognos? If so, here is the solution to fix and retrieve it in a single row.

Reason: Duplicate rows in Cognos reports on comments often arise because of the one-to-many relationship between expense reports and its comments. Since each comment is treated as a distinct record, the primary expense report is repeated for each comment linked to it.

Solution: To fetch entry comments from Concur via Cognos report without creating duplicate records, we must use a method that merges comments into a single field and here are the steps that we need to follow:

Step 1: On the SAP Concur home page click Home > Applications > Reporting > Intelligence.

Step 2: In Concur Reporting tool, click on New> Report from four horizontal lines available at top left corner.

Step 3: Select Blank template and click on Create button from the top right corner.

Step 4: Click on Select a Source+ and then click on Concur Data Warehouse.

Step 5: Click on Open.

Step 6: Expand the Expense folder on the left side and under Expense Reports > Report Header Information, select the required fields and then drag & drop it to the right side.

Step 7: Similarly, under Expense Reports folder> Entry Information, select the required fields and drag & drop it on the right side.

Step 8: On the top, click on Report> Query1 as mentioned in the screenshot.

Step 9: Click on + symbol in Data Items.

Step 10: Double click on the #Entry Key from Entry Information>Keys folder and click on OK. The Entry Key will be added to the Data Items.

 

Step 11: Click on Report> Queries.

Step 12: Drag and Drop the Query icon to the right side under Query 1.

Step 13: Query2 will be added.

Step 14: Double click on Query2.

Step 15: Double click on the Comments (under Report Entry Comments) and #Assiciated Report Entry Key (under Keys folder). The fields will be added to the Data Items.

Step 16: Click on Report> Page1.

Step 17: Under Toolbox icon, double click on Repeater icon.

Step 18: Make sure the Query2 is selected under Query Name and Click OK.

Step 19: The Repeater column is added at the right end of the table.

Under Date Items icon drag and drop the Comment under Query2 to the Repeater column available on the right side.

Step 20: Click on Comments under Repeater Column and select the three vertical dots from the options and then select Mater Detail Relationships.

Step 21: Click on New Link. Make sure the link is connected as mentioned in the screenshot. Click OK

Step 22: Click on Repeater field. Enter ‘Entry Comments’ in the text box and click OK

Step 23: Click on lock icon to unlock

Step 24: From the Toolbox icon. Drag and drop the Text Item to the comment field. Enter , (comma and space) in the Text window and then click OK.

Step 25: Click Lock icon to lock the report.

Step 26: Click on Report> Page1

Step 27: Select Employee and Report Name columns and then click on Group icon as shown in the screenshot.

Step 28: Click on Save icon to save the report. Click on Run button to retrieve the data and multiple comments entered for an expense entry will be appearing in a single row with comma in the output file.

entry.title: File extension docx not allowed for workstation application WRD for DMS object
entry.content:
Hello Experts,

While loading IDOCs I'm getting Workstation application DOX not defined error. Could you please help me?

I have checked in the DC50 also it is maintained in the T-Code I have attached my error screenshot as well as the DC50 T-Code screenshot. 

Please help me out from this issue.

Thanks in advance,

Best Regards,

Prashanth Kumar.Error Diagnosis ScreenshotError after processing IDOCsDC30 Screenshot of *.DOC

entry.title: Concur Invoice Integration to SAP S/4 HANA Error - Posting Failed due to Missing Content.
entry.content:
Hi,

First post on the SAP Community Forum so please forgive me for any errors in posting. I have an issue integrating our Concur Invoice Module to our SAP S/4 HANA instance within our organisation. 

We have activated the standard integration between SAP S/4 HANA and the Concur Invoice module for our organisation via ICS. Currently we are performing our integration test and we are experiencing an error 'Posting failed due to missing content. Please check COMPANY_CODE'. We noticed that the part causing the error is specifically the one related to the allocation, as it seems the company code is missing. We also do not see the Cost Center and the error shown is 'Posting Failed due to Missing Cost Object'. When we have compared this against the JSON file we can see that both the company code as well as the cost center are missing in the section related to the allocation.  Screenshot attached and JSON file code inserted for reference.

Many thanks,
Mitesh

{"requestHeader":{"invoicePayIndicator":"N","amountTax":0E-8,"isTest":"N","vendorInvoiceNumber":"4580362","discountPercentage":null,"reqKey":205,"currencyAlphaCode":"GBP","ledgerCode":"SAP","clearingAccountCode":null,"paymentDueDate":"2025-01-14T00:00:00.000Z","payMethodType":"CLIENT","poNumber":null,"liabilityAccountCode":"","invoiceDate":"2024-12-31T00:00:00.000Z","submitDate":"2025-11-06T17:43:12.123Z","ledgerName":"SAP","processCompleteDate":null,"invoiceReceivedDate":"2025-11-05T00:00:00.000Z","multiplePurchaseOrder":"N","postingDate":null,"requestTotal":538.35000000,"versionId":"4","requestOrgUnit1Value":null,"requestOrgUnit2Value":null,"requestOrgUnit3Value":null,"requestOrgUnit4Value":null,"requestOrgUnit5Value":null,"requestOrgUnit6Value":null,"amountVat1":80.36000000,"amountVat2":0E-8,"amountVat3":0E-8,"amountVat4":0E-8,"requestOrgUnit1Code":null,"requestOrgUnit4Code":null,"requestOrgUnit3Code":null,"requestOrgUnit6Code":null,"requestOrgUnit5Code":null,"requestOrgUnit2Code":null,"invoiceImageId":"419123262EE74BFA901C","revisionNumber":"1","requestId":"419123262EE74BFA901C","deliverySlipNumber":null,"requestCreationDate":"2025-11-06T17:32:27.790Z","amountShippingTotal":0E-8,"netPaymentTermDays":"14","amountNetInvoice":538.35000000,"requestCustom21Code":null,"requestCustom22Code":null,"requestCustom19Value":null,"requestCustom18Value":null,"requestCustom17Value":null,"requestCustom16Value":null,"requestCustom15Value":null,"requestCustom14Value":null,"requestCustom13Value":null,"requestCustom11Value":null,"requestCustom9Value":null,"requestCustom10Value":null,"requestCustom8Value":null,"requestCustom6Value":null,"requestCustom21Value":null,"requestCustom22Value":null,"requestCustom23Value":null,"requestCustom24Value":null,"requestTitle":"Not Sprint (UK) Limited","vendorContactFirstName":null,"vendorContactLastName":null,"vendorRemitToAddressCode":"2000000275","vendorCode":"2000000275","vendorShipFromAddressCode":null},"lineItem":[{"allocation":[{"journal":{"amountShipping":0,"accountCode":"5520000700","amountNet":401.80,"amountGross":482.16},"allocationCustom6Code":null,"allocationCustom7Code":null,"allocationCustom8Code":null,"allocationCustom9Code":null,"allocationCustom10Code":null,"allocationCustom11Code":null,"allocationCustom12Code":null,"allocationCustom13Code":null,"allocationCustom14Code":null,"allocationCustom15Code":null,"allocationCustom16Code":null,"allocationCustom17Code":null,"allocationCustom18Code":null,"allocationCustom19Code":null,"allocationCustom20Code":null,"allocationCustom19Value":null,"allocationCustom18Value":null,"allocationCustom17Value":null,"allocationCustom16Value":null,"allocationCustom15Value":null,"allocationCustom14Value":null,"allocationCustom13Value":null,"allocationCustom11Value":null,"allocationCustom9Value":null,"allocationCustom10Value":null,"allocationCustom8Value":null,"allocationCustom6Value":null,"allocationAccountCode":"5520000700","allocationKey":730,"allocationCustom2Code":null,"allocationCustom3Code":null,"allocationCustom4Code":null,"allocationCustom5Code":null,"allocationCustom1Code":null,"allocationCustom1Value":null,"allocationCustom2Value":null,"allocationCustom4Value":null,"allocationCustom3Value":null,"allocationCustom7Value":null,"allocationCustom12Value":null,"allocationCustom20Value":null,"allocationCustom5Value":null,"allocationPercentage":100.00000000}],"expenseTypeName":"5520000700 entry.title: Validation for only employee without allowance is able to submit transportation claim in Concur
entry.content:
Hi All FI, HCM, SuccessFactors, and Concur Experts.

If we may know,
currently there is an inquiry from the end-user about the feasibility of set-up a kind of validation or restriction rule to only employee that receive "monthly transport allowance" will not be able to submit "taxi/transportation charges" claim via SAP Concur system.

Would you like to kindly help us on giving advice whether there is any specific technical field or information that we could use as such an indicator to know which personel number of employee that receive "monthly transport allowance" in SAP FI or SAP HCM / SuccessFactors? to be further integrated or acknowledged (captured) by the Concur system then.

Thank you so much, and we are looking forward to discussing this with you.

Ani.

@RafaelMira @Ronak_Jhuthawat @jacqting @Michaela_Sedlicka @LeoSupriano @katharina_sorge @yogeshgsalvi @harinarasimha @siddharthrajora 

SAP S/4HANA integration with SAP Concur solutions SAP Concur SAP SuccessFactors Employee Central integration to SAP Business Suite HCM (Human Capital Management) FIN Travel Management

entry.title: Need Assistance: SAC Import Issue Using Travel & Expense – Concur Package
entry.content:
Hi Team,

We are facing an issue with Import Data Access when importing data into SAC using Concur as the source through the SAP package Travel & Expense – Concur (SAP Help Portal). We raised an SAP ticket, and the response indicated that this package is not covered under the SAP Support Package, based on the information from the SAP Help Portal. They advised us to contact sap_analytics_cloud_content@sap.com.

Has anyone else faced a similar issue?

Thank you,

Regards,

Pradeepkumar Deva

EY LLP

entry.title: Setting up Record Type 320 Import
entry.content:
Hello,

Our company currently runs a Record Type of 300 & 350 on the same scheduled Job. Per documentation it discusses running a 320 on a different import. When I am looking at the Import/Extract Monitor I am not seeing an option to add another scheduled job for these 320 imports. 

I am fairly new to Concur and perhaps I am missing something. Just looking for some direction or documentation on how I would go about setting up the 320 import.

 

thanks!

Jake

entry.title: Integration issue between SAP Enable Now (SEN) and SAP Concur
entry.content:
Hi all,
 
We are experiencing an integration issue between SAP Enable Now (SEN) and SAP Concur when using SAP Companion as the help layer within Concur. Therefore your help would be highly appreciated! 
 
Expected Behavior:
When a user clicks the Help icon within Concur, the contextual training content from SAP Enable Now should open directly inside Concur through SAP Companion—without requiring the user to separately open the Enable Now browser window or login.
 
Actual Behavior:
Training content displays only if both Concur and Enable Now are already open in the user’s browser.
  • If the user opens Concur alone and clicks the Help icon, no Enable Now content loads in SAP Companion.
  • The integration appears to require an active browser session with Enable Now, which should not be necessary.
Impact
  • Users cannot seamlessly access contextual help from SAP Companion within Concur.
  • Training materials do not load unless users manually open the Enable Now browser tab first.
  • This breaks the expected single-entry experience and affects productivity and training adoption.

 

Thanks a lot for your help in advance!

entry.title: How Are Users Synced from Concur to GDS After SuccessFactors Integration?
entry.content:
Hi everyone,

I need some clarification on user synchronization between SuccessFactors, Concur, and GDS.

In my scenario, the client has SuccessFactors feeding data into Concur nightly. So when a new employee is added in SuccessFactors, they appear in Concur after that nightly job.

Now, once that new user is in Concur, how does that synchronization to GDS happen? (At the moment I only know two ways:  importing the file ProfileSyncQueue or making a manual change in the user profile) but they works for an initial synchronization,  I don't think I need to do the import every time they hire someone
So, in this case, are they synced automatically, or is that still dependent on a manual import process, or do they need to set something in the configuration? I want to be sure I understand the flow fully. 

 

Thank you!

entry.title: City of purchase does not exit
entry.content:
I have some expenses made in Sankt Anton in Austria, but this location does not exist. 

 

What to do??

 

Thanks!

entry.title: Concur - Similar to ICS, we need budget integration from SAP S/4 with Concur.
entry.content:
hi Team

Concur - Similar to ICS, we need budget integration from SAP S/4 with Concur.

Please tell me how we can achieveit 

Regards

Surjith

entry.title: Using Concur API in Build Proces Automation
entry.content:
Hi, 

in SAP Build Process Automation we're trying to access Concur data via API. 

We tried to run GET Retrieve Users (Identity v4 API) and stated /profile/identity/v4 as Resource Path and received this message: 

"{\"status\":400,\"schemas\":[\"urn:ietf:params:scim:api:messages:2.0:Error\",\"urn:ietf:params:scim:api:messages:concur:2.0:Error\"],\"urn:ietf:params:scim:api:messages:concur:2.0:Error\":{\"messages\":[{\"code\":\"BAD_REQUEST\"}]},\"detail\":\"Company ID is required\"}"

We're assuming that there's something missing within our destination configuration in our BTP subaccount. 
We used the following settings:

Type: HTTP
Proxy Type: Internet
URL: https://eu2.api.concursolutions.com
Authentication: OAuth2ClientCredentials
Client ID
Client Secret
Token Service URL: https://eu2.api.concursolutions.com/oauth2/v0/token
Token Service URL Type: Dedicated

Additional Properties
companyID


In Concur we generated an OAuth2 App with the Grants refresh_token, client_credentials and password and selected all available scopes.

Any hints on how to make this API work are greatly appreciated!

Thanks
Franziska

entry.title: Tax Posting Issue with Sales Tax Payable G/L Account in Concur Integration
entry.content:
Hi SAP Community,

We are facing an issue while posting an invoice from SAP Concur using the G/L account 22002000 (Sales Tax Payable – CO). 

To investigate the problem, we tried reproducing the issue manually in the foreground using the Create Supplier Invoice app. During this process, we encountered the following two issues:

  1. When the tax G/L line item (account 22002000) was entered, the system displayed the error:
    “Please enter a value for field Base Amount for account 22002000.”

  2. To resolve this, we entered a base amount for the tax G/L item. However, after doing so, the system raised another error:
    “Tax entry not possible in this item.”

Could you please review this issue and advise on how it can be resolved? For reference, I have attached screenshots of both error messages.

Note: The G/L account 22002000 is configured with “Posting without tax allowed” = Yes.

Looking forward to your guidance.

Thanks

Naveen Kumar

entry.title: Why Employee termination date is not getting updated in SAP Concur (ICS integration)
entry.content:
Query: Why is the termination date not getting updated in SAP Concur for the employees already terminated in SAP S/4 HANA system while using SAP ICS integration?

Solution: Sometimes while using the SAP ICS integration for Employee Replication from SAP S/4 HANA system to SAP Concur system, the employee’s termination date is not updated in SAP Concur system and their SAP Concur profile stays active though the employee left the company.

There could be several reasons which can causes of this issue. Below are few troubleshooting points which can help to fix this problem.

Employees termination date will be sent from SAP S/4 HANA system to SAP Concur system in the below cases:

  • The employee’s employment status in the SAP S/4 HANA system must be inactive.
  • The employee must already be part of the successful replicated employees from SAP S/4 HANA system to SAP Concur system in past.
  • The employee must be terminated in the SAP S/4 HANA system, and the retention period must be maintained per country/region to export the employee’s termination date to SAP Concur system.
  • The employee replication ‘Specify Field Mapping’ has the ‘Export Enabled” button ticked (Export=YES) for the field mapping of the field termination date.
  • The employee’s termination date must be picked and filled with a correct value in JSON file generated for Employee Replication from SAP S/4 HANA system to SAP Concur system.

entry.title: What is the easiest way to extract SAP Concur and Fieldglass data for contingent workforce spend ana
entry.content:
Procurement manager trying to get a consolidated view of contingent workforce spend. We use fieldglass for contingent labor management and concur for expense reporting but the data lives in two completely separate systems with different data models and different reporting capabilities. Finance wants a single view that shows total contingent workforce cost including both the staffing agency fees in fieldglass and the travel and expense reimbursements in concur for those same workers.

The embedded reporting within each system covers basic operational stuff but it's not sufficient for the kind of cross source analysis finance actually needs. You can't blend fieldglass staffing data with concur expenses with ariba procurement in sap's native reporting, and self service analytics is basically nonexistent within the apps themselves. Joining them externally requires matching worker records across systems that use different identifiers. Fieldglass uses sow ids and worker ids while concur uses employee ids and cost center codes. Some contingent workers are in concur as temporary employees, others file expenses through the agency which shows up differently.

I've looked at using sap analytics cloud to consolidate but getting the data into sac from both systems requires setting up connections that our basis team doesn't have capacity for. We also need this data in our main analytics warehouse alongside other procurement data from ariba for a complete category spend view. Is anyone successfully combining fieldglass and concur data for workforce analytics?

entry.title: Company Car - PY US
entry.content:
Hello - is IT 0442 just for PY - GB purposes? Does Us have its own version or an alternate available.? How is SAP Concur as a solution if we could only get the vehicle mileage record and split in to business & personal use there, then send a report of the mileage to payroll for further calculations?

 

please share your experience or suggestions.

entry.title: Expense disappeared, unable to finish expense report
entry.content:
How do I get the website to remember a document it used to have?

For travel reimbursement, I had a preapproved hotel. When I created an expense report and selected it along with my receipt, the preapproved hotel was not uploaded but was also removed from "available expenses". Now my receipt says I need to upload a preapproved expense, but I have no option to do so.

entry.title: SAP Concur Integration - Financial Posting Flow in CTE_SETUP
entry.content:
This blog explains the sequence followed in SAP CTE_SETUP to process SAP Concur expense reports and move them through the financial posting flow in SAP.

The process is shown from the Financial Posting area of the SAP Concur integration setup, where expense reports are imported, acknowledged, posted, and confirmed back to SAP Concur.

1. Accessing the SAP Concur Integration Setup

Main CTE_SETUP overview screen showing the SAP Concur integration setup and the active Financial Posting area.

 

The process starts in transaction CTE_SETUP, under the screen "Setting Up the Integration with SAP Concur."

In the main overview, the system displays the available integration areas: System, Employee, Cost Center, Financial Posting, and Payroll.

For this process, the relevant area is Financial Posting, because this is where SAP receives, validates, and posts the expense reports coming from SAP Concur.

In the example, the system key is linked to the RFC destination:

CONCUR_QUAL

This RFC destination represents the communication setup between SAP and SAP Concur for the quality or test environment.

The green indicators under Cost Center and Financial Posting show that these integration areas are configured and active.

2. Opening the Financial Posting Menu

Financial Posting dropdown menu showing the available setup and monitoring options.

 

From the main CTE_SETUP screen, open the Financial Posting dropdown.

The available options include:

  • Change Import - used to define or adjust the import behaviour for financial posting documents.
  • Additional Posting Settings - used for complementary posting configuration, depending on the company's posting requirements.
  • Symbolic Accounts - this option appears inactive in the screenshot, meaning it is not currently available or not relevant for this setup.
  • Deviating Currencies - used when specific currency handling is required between Concur and SAP.
  • BAdI Implementations - used for customer-specific logic or enhancements during the posting process.
  • Concur Documents - the key option for monitoring and processing the expense reports received from SAP Concur.

For the posting execution and monitoring flow, select Financial Posting -> Concur Documents.

3. Concur Documents Overview

Concur Documents monitor showing expense reports available for processing.

After selecting Concur Documents, the system opens the document monitor. This screen allows the user to search and manage Concur documents that are available for processing in SAP.

The main selection fields are:

  • System Key - identifies the Concur integration system. In this example: 1 - CONCUR_QUAL.
  • Document Type - defines the type of document to be processed. In this case: Expense Report.
  • View Documents - controls which documents are displayed. In the screenshot, it is set to All.
  • Document ID - can be used to search for one specific expense report document.

After pressing Search, the system displays the available Concur expense reports. In the example, the system shows Total Number: 2.

Both documents are shown with status New. This means the expense reports have not yet completed the posting process in SAP.

4. Manual Processing: Import Documents from Queue

Manual step-by-step option to import documents from the Concur queue.

 
 

For testing or analysis purposes, the process can be executed manually.

From the Feedback Loop menu, select Step-by-Step -> Import Documents from Queue.

This action imports the expense reports from the Concur queue into SAP.

5. Import Documents from Queue Selection

Import Documents from Queue pop-up showing all documents or single document selection.

After selecting Import Documents from Queue, SAP opens a pop-up where the user defines what should be imported.

The pop-up contains the following fields and options:

  • System Key - the Concur system from which the documents will be imported.
  • Document Type - the document category to import. In this case: Expense Report.
  • Detail Messages On Separate Screen - when selected, SAP displays detailed log messages after the import.
  • All Documents - imports all available documents from the queue.
  • Single Document - allows the user to import only one specific expense report by entering the Document ID.

Important remark: manual versus automatic processing

Manual processing is mainly used for testing, troubleshooting, and analysis.

During testing, it is useful to execute each Expense Report ID individually. This allows the consultant or support team to review how each report behaves, validate the mapping, analyse errors, and confirm whether the expected financial document can be created.

However, manual processing should not be the normal operational approach.

The normal basis should be automatic processing, scheduled through background job execution. The frequency of the automatic job depends on each company's business requirements. Some companies may run it every few minutes, while others may schedule it hourly, daily, or aligned with internal expense processing cycles.

6. Import Result and Processing Logs

Display the logs screen showing that the expense reports were successfully imported.

After running Import Documents from Queue, SAP displays the processing log.

In the screenshot, the message is:

Expense Report: Import completed with 2 successful document(s).

This confirms that the two Concur expense reports were successfully imported into SAP.

The log also displays individual entries, such as 47 / 1 and 5 / 1. These entries represent the documents imported during the process.

At this stage, the documents have been brought from the Concur queue into SAP, but they still need to continue through the next steps of the feedback loop.

7. Sending the Acknowledgement

Feedback Loop menu showing the Send Acknowledgement step.

Once the documents are imported, the next step is to send an acknowledgement.

From the Feedback Loop menu, select Step-by-Step -> Send Acknowledgement.

This step informs SAP Concur that SAP has received the document.

After acknowledgement, the document status is updated in the monitor. In the screenshot, the Acknowledgement. The checkbox is marked for the selected expense report. This indicates that the acknowledgement step has been completed.

The purpose of this step is to keep both systems aligned. SAP Concur needs to know that the expense report has been successfully received by SAP before the process continues.

8. Posting the Expense Report

Feedback Loop menu showing the Post step available after acknowledgement.

After the expense report has been imported and acknowledged, the next available step is Step-by-Step -> Post.

This is the actual financial posting step in SAP.

During this step, SAP tries to create the FI document based on the data received from SAP Concur. This can include:

  • Expense report data
  • Employee or vendor assignment
  • Cost center or internal allocation
  • Expense types
  • Tax information
  • Company code
  • Currency
  • G/L account determination
  • Posting date and document date

If all required mapping and master data are correct, SAP creates the accounting document. If something is missing or incorrect, the posting will fail and the error must be analyzed in the log.

Typical issues during this step can include missing employee/vendor mapping, invalid cost center, missing G/L account assignment, incorrect tax code, currency configuration issues, company code not determined, posting period closed, or missing document type/posting configuration.

9. Final Confirmation Back to SAP Concur

After the document is posted successfully, the final feedback step is Send Confirmation.

This step sends the final status back to SAP Concur and tells Concur that the expense report has been successfully processed and posted in SAP.

In the screenshots, Send Confirmation is still inactive because the posting step has not yet been completed for the selected document.

The normal sequence is:

Import Documents from Queue -> Send Acknowledgement -> Post -> Send Confirmation

Only when the previous step is completed does the next step become available.

10. Recommended Operational Path

For productive use, this process should be automated.

The manual step-by-step approach is useful during initial configuration, SIT testing, UAT testing, issue analysis, specific Expense Report ID troubleshooting, validation of financial posting behavior, and checking mapping or master data errors.

For daily operations, the process should run automatically using scheduled background jobs.

The automatic job should handle the normal end-to-end flow without user intervention:

  • Import documents
  • Send acknowledgement
  • Post the FI document
  • Send confirmation back to SAP Concur

The job frequency should be defined per company based on the expected volume and operational requirements. For example, a company with high expense volume may need frequent execution, while another company may only require a few scheduled runs per day.

11. End-to-End Flow Summary

The end-to-end financial posting flow in CTE_SETUP works as follows:

  1. SAP Concur sends the approved expense report to the integration queue.
  2. SAP imports the expense report through Concur Documents.
  3. SAP sends an acknowledgement back to SAP Concur.
  4. SAP posts the financial document.
  5. SAP sends the final confirmation back to SAP Concur.
  6. The document status is updated in both systems.

In summary, the Concur Documents monitor in CTE_SETUP is used to control, test, and troubleshoot the financial posting process for SAP Concur expense reports. Manual processing is helpful during testing and analysis, but the standard operational model should be automatic execution through scheduled jobs.

entry.title: S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition Supports Enhanced WBS Element Search or Not
entry.content:
I found no SAP source stating the Enhanced WBS Element Search feature in Concur is limited to S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition only or unavailable for Private Edition. 

Can I have a confirmation on whether the feature is supported for S/4HANA Cloud Private Edition or not? Thanks.
